rows.llm_rationale: Current date is late April 2026; by year-end there are only ~8 months for 5 cuts. The crowd price of 1.1% appears significantly underpriced relative to baseline Fed cutting cycles and CME FedWatch data typical in late Q2. Even in dovish scenarios, 5 cuts in 8 months requires sustained economic weakness. However, tail risks (recession, financial stress) make this non-trivial. Adjusting upward from crowd price to ~8% to reflect realistic tail-scenario probability while remaining skeptical of such an aggressive cutting pace.
rows.question: Will 5 Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?
